statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: First line n, second line queue elements (space-separated)
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(1), Space complexity: O(n)

hints.text: Check the implementation of the dequeue method

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing the Broken Queue Dequeue Method
editorial.intuition: The queue's front element is not being properly dequeued
editorial.bruteForce: Current implementation is a simple O(n) traversal to find and remove the front element
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to keep track of the front element separately and update it on each enqueue/dequeue operation
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness proof involves showing that the dequeued element is always the correct one
editorial.dryRun: A step-by-step example run would involve demonstrating how the queue's state changes after each enqueue/dequeue operation
commonMistakes: not keeping track of the front element correctly
